# Transcrição: 6. Questions Not to Ask

- **Fonte:** 6. Questions Not to Ask
- **Duração:** 01:47
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:47]

Okay, one of the reasons people quit fact-finding and quit qualifying and quit collecting information on customers is they get their hand. You know, hey, stop asking me that because you're asking bad questions. So I want to give you a list of bad questions. What can you put down? What can you pay? What do you owe? What is your budget? Will you buy today? Will you be trading something in? How's your credit? Where do you work? How long have you been there? What do you make on your job? Is your spouse going to be involved in a decision? Oh my gosh, you're going to kill this guy. Do you rent or own your home? Are you shopping anywhere else? What price did they give you? Look, these questions are negative. What are the negatives of asking these types of qualifying questions? How much down? How much a month? What do you owe? What will you do? Have you been shopping? Look, your buyer is going to lock in. First of all, if you're asking for figures, prices, and payments in terms and what they'll do, they're going to lock down on a figure that's too low. They're going to set a target that's unachievable. The answers are given prematurely before they're even sold on the fact that the product will solve their problems and that they love it. Your buyer is going to minimize any figures they're going to pay because they don't like you yet. There's no relationship. And the salesperson, you, are going to become negatively affected because the answers you receive are going to be so unrealistic. The customer is going to become unrealistic. You're going to lose your mind. They're going to become inflexible because they're going to lock down. It's going to make your closing your negotiations very difficult. And the other reason you don't want to ask those questions is it makes you sound like everyone else they'll shop. Stay away from these bad questions. Stay away from questions that don't get you smart answers, that don't further you down the road to the sale and then actually stop things and cause people to think that you're just like everybody else.
